decisions.title: Release gRPC API to production
decisions.context: ENG-9401 and ENG-9070 show gRPC API is being finalized. Need to decide on release timing and feature completeness vs. iterative rollout for Embed Deeper pillar.
decisions.blockedBy: Policy API validation endpoint (ENG-9398) required before user-facing release

arguments.for: API foundation enables deeper embeddings, multiple teams ready to integrate, technical work complete
arguments.against: Missing validation endpoint creates risk of invalid configs in production, could damage customer trust

decisions.title: Prioritize PyRunner production stability vs. new features
decisions.context: ENG-9056 shows PyRunner pods unavailable in prod while ENG-9337/9406 show Files in CEL under development. Python Rules is key 2026 product but infrastructure unstable.

arguments.for: Nail the Core requires production stability first, customer decisions depend on reliable execution
arguments.against: Files in CEL represents major capability expansion, delay risks competitive position

decisions.title: Ship Live Alerts new execution engine
decisions.context: ENG-8906 RFC for new execution engine, ENG-8930 Kafka topic configured. Critical for real-time decisions (North Star metric) but architectural change carries risk.

arguments.for: Enables faster signal-to-decision loop, unlocks scalability for Embed Deeper, infrastructure modernization supports Build the Moat
arguments.against: RFC stage suggests design not final, dual systems during migration increases complexity

decisions.title: Address UX debt vs. new feature velocity
decisions.context: Multiple UX polish issues (ENG-9379, ENG-9373, ENG-9370, ENG-9106, ENG-9235 analytics) alongside DES-55 family considerations. Quality vs. speed tradeoff.

arguments.for: Nail the Core means polished experiences increase decisions captured, analytics gaps prevent measuring success
arguments.against: Feature velocity drives customer value faster, UX issues are non-blocking workarounds exist

decisions.title: Invest in Sift Edge observability now or later
decisions.context: ENG-8858 Sift Edge Metrics in flight. Edge is 2026 product but metrics infrastructure competes with feature development. Build the Moat through operational excellence.

arguments.for: Cannot embed deeper without visibility into edge performance, metrics enable proactive support reducing churn
arguments.against: Edge adoption low, metrics won't drive decisions until customer base larger, premature optimization

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load (80% of time) limiting execution bandwidth. gRPC finalization, PyRunner stability, and UX polish require focused eng time.
alerts: 5 open decisions with deadlines next 2 weeks - need meeting to unblock
PyRunner prod issue requires immediate focus time allocation
UX debt accumulating faster than resolution - consider dedicated sprint
